comments.text: The second problem listed was solved in a (rather moving) paper of Sárközy in 1999 ("Complete tripartite subgraphs in the coprime graph of integers", Discrete mathematics, 202(1-3), pp.227-238). He shows that one can take $\ell\gtrsim \frac{\log n}{\log\log\log n}$. (The reference was located by GPT-5.) (The site has been updated to address this comment.)
why was the paper "rather moving"? and what does that mean here?

Quoting from the paper, Erdős wrote this a few days before he died: "We hope to return to this problem in the near future (if there is a future for me), but Sárközy will surely return to these problems”. Sárközy therefore wrote: "Sadly, due to Paul’s death, we have not been able to continue the work jointly. It is a tremendous honor for me to fulfill Paul’s moving prediction with this paper.”
